commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer review, checks, and sequencing with the companion producer PR.; Cleared: No security or supply-chain concern found; the diff is limited to TypeScript event forwarding, channel formatting, and tests with no dependency, workflow, package, secret, permission, or downloaded-code changes. (sha=4c466b1a9b95106623af04dd606e4ee6ae7e3499)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared CLI commentary bridge from item/preamble agent events to channel progress callbacks, plus full-length commentary rendering and regression coverage for primary and queued CLI runs.

PR surface: Source +64, Tests +129. Total +193 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main has no item/preamble subscriber in runCliAgentWithLifecycle, so a CLI backend that emits that event on the agent-event bus cannot reach opts.onItemEvent from this path. The branch's primary and queued tests model that missing delivery path directly.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer review, checks, and sequencing with the companion producer PR.

target.title: fix(cli): bridge inter-tool commentary events to channel progress
target.body:
## Summary

Two related fixes to **CLI-runtime inter-tool commentary** in channel progress drafts:

1. **Delivery bridge** — the missing subscriber in the shared CLI-runtime dispatch (`runCliAgentWithLifecycle`). Inter-tool commentary surfaces on the agent-event bus as `stream:"item", kind:"preamble"`, but only the assistant/reasoning/tool streams were bridged to the channel — the item/preamble stream had **no subscriber**, so commentary was dropped. This adds `createCommentaryEventBridge` → `onItemEvent` (both the primary and queued follow-up call sites).
2. **Render in full (noCompact)** — commentary lines are assistant *prose* and must not be compacted like tool lines (file paths/args). They now carry `noCompact` so the progress-draft renderer skips per-line compaction; long commentary renders fully and spills to a new message at the channel limit rather than truncating mid-sentence.

This is **general CLI-runtime infrastructure** (claude-cli, google-gemini-cli, …), not claude-specific — it brings the CLI dispatch to parity with codex's ACP commentary. The first producer on this path is **claude-cli (#89834)**; together they close #87326 for claude-cli.

## Tests

- `tsgo` clean
- `bridges CLI commentary agent events into onItemEvent for live preview` (primary + queued follow-up)

## Proof (combined stack: this + #89834 producer)

Captured via a local recreation of the Mantis (telegram-desktop-proof) flow on crabbox.

Inter-tool commentary appears in the live progress draft (transient in non-persist mode — the recording is the evidence):

![claude-cli commentary in the live progress draft](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof/commentary-persist-v2/commentary-transient.gif)

**noCompact** — a long (>120-char) multi-sentence commentary line rendering in **full** (here shown in the persisted draft from the #89850 stack, same renderer):

![long commentary rendered in full](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof/commentary-persist-v2/persist-accumulate.png)

## Issues

Part of the fix for #87326 (delivery + render half; **depends on #89834** for the claude-cli producer).
Related: #84486.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
